text: Pro-life isn’t a slogan—it’s defending the innocent when the world looks away. Until politicians stop funding abortion, real “compassion” is just talk.
Abortion isn’t healthcare. It’s the killing of a vulnerable child, dressed up with euphemisms. Stand for life—no exceptions.
Planned Parenthood’s mask keeps slipping: they call it “choice,” but silence and coercion aren’t rights. Women deserve better than profit-driven death.
If life begins at conception, how can anyone call killing unborn children “moral progress”? The facts don’t change—only the excuses do.
“Rescue those who are unjustly sentenced to die.” (Proverbs 24:11) Abortion is that unjust sentence—pray, vote, and speak up.
Pro-abortion arguments collapse on one question: if it’s not a human life, why does the “procedure” end a beating heart?
The abortion pill is still abortion—just industrialized into a prescription. Pro-life means fighting the whole pipeline, not celebrating headlines.
Catholic leaders: deny communion to abortion-supporting politicians. Real faith requires real accountability—no more tolerance for legalized killing.
They’ll claim they’re “protecting women,” yet the solution is to end a pregnancy. Real protection is care, support, and shelter—not death.
Equating unborn children to inconvenience is moral failure. Pray for repentance—and demand laws that protect the smallest among us.
Abortion is not “healthcare,” it’s the killing of the innocent. If a child can’t be safe in the womb, society has failed the most basic test of justice. God help us choose life—clearly and loudly.
Abortion isn’t “healthcare”—it’s the killing of the innocent. If we can defend the vulnerable outside the womb, we can’t pretend they’re invisible inside it. Legalize mercy, not murder.

Abortion isn’t “health care.” It’s killing an innocent child.

If our laws protect the born, they must protect the unborn too—no loopholes, no euphemisms, no compromises.

Abortion isn’t “healthcare,” it’s violence against the innocent. If churches truly believe every life is made by God, then denying communion to pro-abortion politicians must start now—no excuses, no delay.
Stop pretending abortion is complicated. Science and biology don’t change with politics: a living human being is present from conception—so stop calling murder “choice.”
“No one says ‘it’s not my job’ when a child is in danger.” Why is it acceptable to stand by while unborn children are killed? Pro-life means acting, not posting.
Planned Parenthood closing some locations doesn’t end abortion. Pills are the new front—expose the lies, defund the mills, and protect moms and babies with real help.
Catholic leaders: either you defend the unborn or you keep rewarding pro-abortion politicians. If communion is reserved for the holy, then stop tolerating the openly murderous.
The argument that “it’s only a clump of cells” collapses under one question: when does life begin for you? If it begins at conception, you can’t justify killing it.
Equating abortion with the death penalty is still morally upside down—one punishes crime, the other kills the innocent. Scripture defends the unjustly sentenced, not the unborn destroyed.
If you truly believe in justice, stop treating abortion like a debate topic. Laws should protect the weakest—the child in the womb—because they can’t vote or fight back.
A nation that funds abortion while claiming to value women is lying. Women deserve support, not the funnel to chemical coercion and permanent loss.
Pro-life isn’t a slogan—it’s a responsibility. Pray, protest, and push for protections that end abortion, including the abortion pill pipeline that’s spreading fast.

Abortion isn’t “choice” — it’s the taking of a human life.

Stop funding the mills, stop calling it healthcare, and demand real protection for the unborn and their families.
Abortion isn’t a “choice” once an innocent child is being killed. Calling it anything else won’t change the facts—innocent life ends, and we’re responsible for what we allow.
Abortion isn’t “healthcare”—it’s the intentional ending of innocent life. If we truly believe every human is made in God’s image, then laws must protect the unborn, not fund their killing.
Pro-abortion talking points always dodge the heart of it: a child is a child, from conception onward. Don’t hide behind euphemisms—call it what it is: murder.
If Catholics keep funding politicians who support abortion, the culture won’t change. Hold the line: deny communion to pro-abortion candidates and stop treating conscience like politics.
The “choice” argument never answers the simple question—who gets to choose for the unborn? Nobody. Abortion is the powerful deciding for the powerless.
Abortion is a violence that targets the defenseless. Scripture calls us to rescue the unjustly condemned. Stop calling killing “care” and start protecting life.
Planned Parenthood claims “women’s health,” but the result is death for the unborn. Real compassion looks like saving lives, not manufacturing them into statistics.
Equating abortion with the death penalty is spiritual madness. The death penalty involves due process; abortion is a premeditated killing done without justice.
Abortion ends futures. It’s not a clump of tissue—it’s the beginning of a human life. Defend the innocent, because the unborn can’t speak for themselves.
The goal shouldn’t be debating morality—it should be protecting children. Every day abortion remains legal, we normalize the killing of the innocent.
Pro-life doesn’t mean “pro-pain”—it means pro-human dignity. Provide real support for mothers and babies, and end the legalized slaughter.

Abortion is not “healthcare.” It’s the intentional ending of an innocent life.

No exceptions, no fine print—just murder needing legal consequences and compassion that fights for the mother and child.

Abortion is not “medical care.” It’s the taking of an innocent life.

If society truly valued human dignity, we wouldn’t hide it behind euphemisms or bills—we’d protect the unborn.

“Rescue those who are unjustly sentenced to die” (Proverbs 24:11).

How can anyone call abortion compassionate when it’s a child condemned before birth?

Don’t pretend the abortion industry is about women.
It’s about profit, power, and control—then it’s sold as “choice” to protect the practice.

If you can mourn the death penalty but ignore the unborn, your moral logic is broken.
Defend the innocent—every life is not negotiable.
The unborn aren’t “potential.” They are human beings from conception—alive, growing, and worthy of protection from the first heartbeat.

Church leaders: stop offering polite words while pro-abortion politicians keep funding clinics.
Communion should come with real accountability.

A nation can close buildings and still keep murdering with pills.
If you’re serious about ending abortion, fight the abortion pill too—no excuses.

Pro-life isn’t a slogan.
It’s laws that protect children, compassion for families, and a refusal to normalize killing.

They say “it’s complicated,” but the truth isn’t.
A baby’s life is ended—period. Call it what it is and fight it.

Planned Parenthood may change names, but the mission is the same: end lives in the womb.
Enough with the PR—support real alternatives for women.
